definition.en: Framework 2 is the psychological medium in which the consciousness of the world exists.
definition.zh: 架构二是世界意识存在的心理媒介。

definition.en: Framework 2 is the medium in which your world exists. It represents the vaster psychological reality in which your own subjective life resides.
definition.zh: 架构二是你的世界存在的媒介。它代表着你自己的主观生活所栖居的更广阔的心理实相。

definition.en: Framework 2 is not neutral, but automatically inclined toward what we will here term good or constructive developments. It is a growth medium.
definition.zh: 架构二并非中立，而是自动倾向于我们在此所称的善或建设性的发展。它是一个成长的媒介。

definition.en: Framework 2 is on the one hand an invisible version of the physical universe. On the other hand, however, it is far more than that, for it contains within it probable variations of that universe.
definition.zh: 架构二一方面是有形宇宙的一个不可见版本。但另一方面，它远不止如此，因为它在其内包含了那个宇宙的可能变体。

definition.en: Your inner egos populate Framework 2, and deal with the actual creation of those events that are then objectified.
definition.zh: 你们的内在自我栖居于架构二，并处理那些随后被客观化的事件的实际创造。

explanation:
架构二是所有物质事件发生的心理源头。它是一个无形的、多维度的创造场域，你的思想、情感和信念在这里以电磁属性存在并相互混合，形成世界事件背后的心理气候。架构二并非中性，而是天然倾向于建设性、创造性的发展，积极的思想比消极的更容易在此显化为现实。

架构一（物质世界）完全浸没在架构二中，你的身体在架构一中的持续更新，正是因为它在架构二中有同时存在的实相。架构二中的内我（inner ego）不受因果限制，以更快的推理方式运作，处理着概率和事件的创造性发明。你视为巧合或偶然的事件，实际上是架构二中惊人组织与沟通的结果。

许多架构二的实相无法直接翻译为架构一的事实，而是通过艺术象征和梦境呈现。

quotes.text: "Any event, therefore, has an invisible thickness, a multidimensional basis. Your skies are filled with breezes, currents, clouds, sunlight, dust particles and so forth. The sky vaults above the entire planet. The invisible [vault of] Framework 2 contains endless patterns that change as, say, clouds do -- that mix and merge to form your psychological climate. Thoughts have what we will for now term electromagnetic properties. In those terms your thoughts mix and match with others in Framework 2, creating mass patterns that form the overall psychological basis behind world events. Again, however, Framework 2 is not neutral, but automatically inclined toward what we will here term good or constructive developments. It is a growth medium. Constructive or 'positive' feelings or thoughts are more easily materialized than 'negative' ones because they are in keeping with Framework 2's characteristics."
quotes.translation: 因此，任何事件都具有一种不可见的厚度，一个多维的基础。你们的天空充满了微风、气流、云朵、阳光、尘埃微粒等等。苍穹笼罩着整个星球。框架2那不可见的穹顶包含着无尽的模式，它们像云朵一样变化——混合交融，形成你们的心理气候。思想具有我们暂且称之为电磁特性的属性。用这些术语来说，你们的思想在框架2中与其他思想混合匹配，创造出大规模的模式，这些模式构成了世界事件背后的整体心理基础。然而，再次强调，框架2并非中立，而是自动倾向于我们在此称之为善的或建设性的发展。它是一个成长媒介。建设性或“正面”的情感或思想比“负面”的更容易物质化，因为它们与框架2的特性相符。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: So far, I have been speaking of Frameworks 1 and 2 separately, and I will continue to do so for your convenience and understanding. Actually the two merge, of course, for your Framework 1 existence is immersed in Framework 2. Again, your body itself is constantly replenished in Framework 1 because of its simultaneous reality in Framework 2. Framework 2 is ever exteriorizing itself, appearing in your experience as Framework 1. You concentrate so thoroughly upon exterior reality, however, that you often ignore the quite apparent deeper sources of your own physical existence. As a result you deal with methods of division and categorization so completely that you lose sight of associative organizations, even though you use them constantly in your own most intimate thought processes.
quotes.translation: 到目前为止，我一直分开谈论框架1和框架2，为了你们的方便和理解，我会继续这样做。实际上，这两者当然是融合的，因为你们框架1的存在是沉浸于框架2之中的。再次强调，你们的身体本身在框架1中不断得到补充，是因为它在框架2中同时存在。框架2不断地外化自身，在你们的经验中显现为框架1。然而，你们如此彻底地专注于外在现实，以至于常常忽略了自己物质存在那相当明显的深层源头。结果，你们如此完全地处理分割和分类的方法，以至于忽视了关联性的组织，即使你们在自己最私密的思想过程中也经常使用它们。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 825, MARCH 6, 1978
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I said that the inner ego reasons, but its reasoning is not restricted to the cause-and-effect limitations that you apply to the reasoning process. The action of the inner ego within the wider sphere of Framework 2 explains many events and seeming coincidences that otherwise seem to make no sense within your world. Many realities within Framework 2 cannot suitably be explained as facts to you in Framework 1, simply because they involve psychological thicknesses that cannot be translated into facts as you think of them. These often appear in the symbolic language of the arts instead, and many of your dreams are translations in which the events of Framework 2 appear in symbolic form.
quotes.translation: 我说过内在自我会推理，但它的推理并不受限于你们应用于推理过程的因果限制。内在自我在框架2更广阔领域内的行动，解释了在你们世界中似乎毫无意义的许多事件和看似巧合的事情。框架2中的许多现实无法作为事实适当地向你们在框架1中解释，仅仅是因为它们涉及无法转化为你们所认为的事实的心理厚度。这些现实反而常常以艺术的象征语言出现，而你们的许多梦就是翻译，其中框架2的事件以象征形式呈现。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: All of those characteristics have their sources in Framework 2, for the psychological medium in Framework 2 is automatically conducive to creativity. It is not simply a neutral dimension, therefore, but contains within itself an automatic predisposition toward the fulfillment of all patterns inherent within it. As James said in Ruburt's book,1 "The universe is of good intent." It is automatically predisposed, again, toward the creation of "good" events. I put the word "good" in quotes for now because of your misconceptions about the nature of good and evil, which we will discuss somewhat later.2
quotes.translation: 所有这些特性都源于框架2，因为框架2中的心理媒介自动有利于创造力。因此，它不仅仅是一个中性的维度，其自身内部还包含一种自动的倾向，以实现其内在的所有模式。正如詹姆斯在鲁柏的书中所说：“宇宙怀有善意。”它再次自动倾向于创造“善”的事件。我暂时将“善”这个词放在引号里，是因为你们对善恶本质的误解，我们稍后会讨论这一点。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 825, MARCH 6, 1978
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Your world is populated by individuals concentrating upon physical activities, dealing with events that are "finished products" -- at least in usual terms. Your inner egos populate Framework 2, and deal with the actual creation of those events that are then objectified. Since "the rules" of Framework 2 are different, that reality is not at all bound by your physical assumptions. It contains, therefore, the inner ego of each individual who has lived or will ever live upon the earth.
quotes.translation: 你们的世界充满了专注于物质活动的个体，他们处理着“成品”般的事件——至少按通常的说法是如此。你们的内在自我则存在于框架2中，处理着那些随后被客观化的事件的实际创造。由于框架2的“规则”不同，那个现实完全不受你们物质假设的束缚。因此，它包含了每一个曾经或将要生活在地球上的个体的内在自我。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978
9:27 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Once scientists theorized the ether as the medium in which the physical universe existed.1 Framework 2 is the psychological medium in which the consciousness of the world exists. The word "ego" is much bandied about, and in many circles it has a poor reputation. It is, however, as I use it, a term meant to express the ordinarily conscious directive portion of the self. It is your conscious version of what you are -- an excellent description, if I do say so myself (with amusement). It is directed outward into the physical world. It is also aware, however, of some of your "unconscious" activities. It is the you you identify with, so it is as aware of your dreams, for example, as you are, and it is quite conscious of the fact that its existence rests upon knowledge that it does not itself possess.
quotes.translation: 曾经，科学家们假设以太是物质宇宙存在的媒介。框架2则是世界意识存在的心理媒介。“自我”这个词被广泛使用，在许多圈子里名声不佳。然而，正如我所使用的，它是一个术语，旨在表达自我通常有意识的指导部分。它是你们对自己有意识版本的认识——如果我可以这么说的话（带着幽默），这是一个极好的描述。它向外指向物质世界。然而，它也意识到你们的一些“无意识”活动。它是你们所认同的那个“你”，因此，例如，它和你们一样意识到你们的梦，并且它很清楚自己的存在依赖于它自身并不拥有的知识。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978
9:27 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Jung's collective unconscious was an attempt to give your world its psychological roots, but Jung1 could not perceive the clarity, organization, and deeper context in which that collective unconscious has its own existence. Reality as Framework 2 is organized in a different fashion than it is in the Framework 1 world, and the processes of reasoning are far quicker. In Framework 1 the reasoning processes work largely by deduction, and they must constantly check their own results against the seemingly concrete experience of physical events. The reasoning of the inner ego is involved with the creative invention of those experiences. It is involved with events in a context of a different kind, for it deals intimately with probabilities.
quotes.translation: 荣格的集体无意识试图为你们的世界赋予心理根源，但荣格未能看清集体无意识自身存在所依托的清晰性、组织性与更深层脉络。实相作为框架二，其组织方式与框架一世界截然不同，推理过程也迅捷得多。在框架一中，推理过程主要依靠演绎法运作，必须不断以物理事件看似具体的经验来检验自身结论。而内在自我的推理则参与这些经验的创造性发明，它在一个不同性质的脉络中与事件打交道，因为其运作与可能性紧密相连。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You may have become a part of the drama of a natural disaster, or avoided it as a result of other seemingly chance occurrences. What appears to you as chance or coincidence, however, is actually the result of the amazing organizations and communications active in the psychological reality of Framework 2. Again, you form your reality -- but how? And how do private existences touch each other, resulting in world events? Before we go any further, then, we must look into the nature of Framework 2.
quotes.translation: 你或许已成为某场自然灾害戏剧的一部分，或因其他看似偶然的事件而避开了它。然而，在你眼中视为机缘巧合的现象，实则是框架二心理实相中活跃的惊人组织与沟通的结果。再次强调，你构建了自己的实相——但如何构建？私人存在又如何相互触及，从而引发世界性事件？因此，在进一步探讨之前，我们必须审视框架二的本质。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: As you have an ego, fully conscious, directed toward the physical world, you also have what I call an inner ego, directed toward inner reality. You have, in other words, a portion of yourself that is fully conscious in Framework 2. The ego in your ordinary world, which again we will call Framework 1, is uniquely equipped to deal with that environment. It manipulates with rules of cause and effect and consecutive moments. It deals with an objectified reality. It can stretch its capacities, becoming far more aware of inner events than it is normally allowed to do, but its main purpose is to deal with the world of effects, to encounter events.
quotes.translation: 正如你们拥有一个完全清醒、面向物质世界的自我，你们也拥有我所谓的面向内在实相的内在自我。换言之，你们有一部分自身在框架二中是完全清醒的。在你们寻常世界（我们仍称之为框架一）中的自我，拥有应对该环境的独特装备。它遵循因果律与连续时刻的规则运作，处理着客体化的实相。它虽能拓展自身能力，比通常允许的程度更敏锐地觉察内在事件，但其主要目的是处理结果的世界，去遭遇事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978
9:27 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: All being is manifestation of energy -- an emotional manifestation of energy. Man can interpret the weather in terms of air pressure and wind currents. He can look to fault lines in an effort to understand earthquakes. All of this works at a certain level, to a certain degree. Man's psyche, however, is emotionally not only a part of his physical environment, but intimately connected with all of nature's manifestations. Using the terms begun in the last chapter, I will say then that man's emotional identification with nature is a strongly-felt reality in Framework 2. And there we must look for the answers regarding man's relationship with nature. There in Framework 2 the nature of the psyche appears quite clearly, so that its sweeps and rhythms can be understood. The manifestations of physical energy follow emotional rhythms that cannot be ascertained with gadgets or instruments, however fine.
quotes.translation: 一切存在皆是能量的显化——一种情感性的能量显化。人类可以用气压与气流来解释天气，可以寻找断层线来理解地震。这些方法在特定层面、特定程度上是有效的。然而，人类的心灵不仅在情感上是其物质环境的一部分，更与所有自然显化紧密相连。沿用上一章提出的术语，我要说：在框架二中，人类对自然的情感认同是一种强烈感知的实相。我们必须在那里寻找关于人类与自然关系的答案。在框架二中，心灵的本质清晰显现，其起伏与韵律得以被理解。物质能量的显化遵循着情感韵律，这些韵律无法用任何精巧的仪器或工具来测定。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: They cast their light over historical events because they are responsible for those events. They mix and merge the inner, unseen but felt, eternal psychic experience of man with the temporal events of his physical days, and form a combination that structures thoughts and beliefs from civilization to civilization. In Framework 2 the interior power of nature is ever-changing. The dreams, hopes, aspirations and fears of man interact in a constant motion that then forms the events of your world. That interaction includes not only man, of course, but the emotional reality of all earthly consciousnesses as well, from a microbe to a scholar, from a frog to a star. You interpret the phenomena of your world according to the mythic characteristics that you have accepted. You organize physical reality, then, through ideas. You use only those perceptions that serve to give those ideas validity. The physical body itself is quite capable of putting the world together in different fashions than the one that is familiar to you.
quotes.translation: 它们将光芒投射于历史事件之上，因为正是它们造就了这些事件。它们将人类内在的、不可见却可感知的、永恒的心灵体验，与其物质岁月中的短暂事件混合交融，形成一种组合，从而构建了从文明到文明的思想与信念。在框架二中，自然的内在力量永恒变化。人类的梦想、希望、渴望与恐惧在持续运动中相互作用，继而形成你们世界的事件。这种互动当然不仅包含人类，也包含从微生物到学者、从青蛙到星辰的一切大地意识的情感实相。你们根据自己所接受的神话特征来解释世界现象。因此，你们通过观念来组织物质实相。你们只运用那些能赋予这些观念有效性的感知。物质身体本身完全有能力以不同于你们所熟悉的方式将世界组合起来。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Before children are acquainted with conventional ideas of guilt and punishment, they realize that it is easier to bring about good events, through wishing, than it is to bring about unhappy ones. The child carries with him [or her] the impetus and supporting energy provided him at birth from Framework 2, and he knows intuitively that desires conducive to his development "happen" easier than those that are not. His natural impulses naturally lead him toward the development of his body and mind, and he is aware of a cushioning effect and support as he acts in accordance with those inner impulses. The child is innately honest. When he gets sick he intuitively knows the reason why, and he knows quite well that he brought about the illness.
quotes.translation: 在孩子们了解关于罪恶与惩罚的传统观念之前，他们就意识到通过愿望带来好事比带来坏事更容易。孩子携带着出生时从框架二获得的动力与支持性能量，他们直觉地知道，有利于自身发展的愿望比那些不利的愿望"更容易实现"。他们的自然冲动自然而然地引导他们发展身心，当他们遵循这些内在冲动行事时，能感受到一种缓冲效应与支持。孩子天生诚实。当他们生病时，他们直觉地知道原因，并且很清楚是自己导致了这场疾病。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 824, MARCH 1, 1978
9:40 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: On the other hand your intuitions follow a different kind of organization, as does your imagination -- one involved with associations, an organization that unifies diverse elements and brings even known events together in a kind of unity that is often innocent of the limitations dictated by cause and effect. In those terms, then, Framework 2 deals with associations, so that within it the recognizable events of the physical world can be put together in an infinite number of ways, after which they appear in your private experience according to directions you have given them through those associations that you form mentally.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，你的直觉遵循着一种不同的组织方式，你的想象力也是如此——这种组织方式涉及联想，将不同的元素统一起来，甚至将已知的事件以一种通常不受因果限制的方式结合在一起。因此，从这些角度来看，框架二处理的是联想，使得物理世界中可识别的事件可以以无限多种方式组合在一起，然后根据你通过心智形成的那些联想所给予的方向，出现在你的个人经验中。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 825, MARCH 6, 1978
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: When you were both intensely involved in your projects, just finished, you let much of your inner experience slide, relatively speaking. The two of you operating together, however, then came up with an idea -- an important one -- that allows you to interpret the Framework 2 material in your own ways. You had instant feedback -- the interplay of a creative nature between the two of you involving your dreams and the camera,3 and so forth. You were each struck by the magical ease with which you seemed, certainly, to perceive and act upon information -- information that you did not even realize you possessed.
quotes.translation: 当你们俩都全神贯注于刚刚完成的计划时，相对而言，你们让许多内在经验溜走了。然而，你们俩一起运作时，却想出了一个重要的主意——一个让你们以自己的方式诠释框架二资料的主意。你们得到了即时的反馈——你们之间涉及梦境和相机等事物的创造性互动。你们各自都被那种神奇的轻松感所震撼，似乎确实能够感知并依据信息行动——这些信息你们甚至没有意识到自己拥有。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Reasoning by itself can only deal with deductions made about the known world. It cannot accept knowledge that comes from "elsewhere," for such information will not fit in reason's categories, and confounds its cause-and-effect patterning. The power to reason comes from Framework 2. In the terms of this discussion, you are able to reason as a result of "magical" events that make reason itself possible. The term "magic" has in one way or another been used to simply describe events for which reason has no answer -- events that exist outside of the framework in which reason feels comfortable.
quotes.translation: 仅凭推理只能处理关于已知世界的推论。它无法接受来自"别处"的知识，因为这类信息不符合理性的分类，并扰乱了其因果模式。推理的力量来自框架二。就本次讨论而言，你们之所以能够推理，是因为那些使推理本身成为可能的"神奇"事件。"神奇"一词在某种程度上一直被用来简单地描述理性无法解释的事件——这些事件存在于理性感到舒适的框架之外。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Dictation: You must understand that in a manner of speaking, Framework 2 is on the one hand an invisible version of the physical universe. On the other hand, however, it is far more than that, for it contains within it probable variations of that universe -- from the most cosmic scale, say, down to probable versions of the most minute events of any given physical day.
quotes.translation: 口授：你必须明白，从某种意义上说，框架二一方面是物理宇宙的一个无形版本。然而，另一方面，它远不止于此，因为它包含了那个宇宙的可能变体——从最宏大的宇宙尺度，比如说，一直到任何特定物理日子里最微小事件的可能版本。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 826, MARCH 8, 1978
9:35 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: He thought of that realm as eternal and unchanging, a perfect but frozen composite that must indeed inspire men toward achievement on the one hand, and on the other reproach them for their failure, since their achievements must necessarily seem puny in contrast. Plato then saw Framework 2 as a splendid, absolute model in which all the works of man had their initial source. Man himself, according to this concept, could not affect that ideal world one whit. He could, however, use it as a source of inspiration.
quotes.translation: 他认为那个领域是永恒不变的，一个完美但僵化的综合体，一方面必然激励人类取得成就，另一方面又因他们的失败而责备他们，因为他们的成就相比之下必然显得微不足道。因此，柏拉图将框架二视为一个辉煌的绝对模型，人类的所有作品都源于此。根据这一概念，人类本身丝毫无法影响那个理想世界。然而，他可以将其作为灵感的源泉。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978

quotes.text: In Framework 2 thoughts instantly form patterns. They are the "natural elements" in that psychological environment that mix, merge, and combine to form, if you will, the psychological cells, atoms, and molecules that compose events. In those terms, the physical events that you perceive or experience can be compared to "psychological objects" that appear to exist with a physical concreteness in space and time. Such events usually seem to begin somewhere in space and time, and clearly end there as well.
quotes.translation: 在框架二中，思想瞬间形成模式。它们是那个心理环境中的"自然元素"，混合、融合并结合，如果你愿意这么说的话，形成构成事件的心理细胞、原子和分子。从这些角度来看，你所感知或体验的物理事件可以比作"心理物体"，它们在空间和时间中似乎以物理的具体性存在。这类事件通常似乎在空间和时间的某处开始，并同样清晰地在那里结束。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 826, MARCH 8, 1978
9:35 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Your Framework 1 life is, again, based on the idea that you have only so much energy, that you will wear out, and that a certain expenditure of energy will produce a given amount of work -- in other words, that applied effort of a certain kind will produce the best results. In the same way, it is believed that the energy of the universe will die out. All of this presupposes "the fact" that no new energy is inserted into the world. The source of the world would therefore seem no longer to exist, having worn itself out in the effort to produce physical phenomena. In the light of such thinking, Framework 2 would be an impossibility.
quotes.translation: 你的框架一生活再次基于这样的观念：你只有有限的能量，你会耗尽，而一定量的能量消耗会产生特定量的工作——换言之，某种特定类型的应用努力会产生最佳结果。同样地，人们相信宇宙的能量也会耗尽。所有这些都预设了"事实"：没有新能量被注入世界。因此，世界的源头似乎不再存在，它已在产生物理现象的努力中耗尽了自己。在这种思维下，框架二是不可能的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You will ignore as information certain stimuli that another person, for example, will latch on to immediately. Even in your own world, then, your interests and desires serve as organizational processes that screen out certain information. The information available in Framework 2 is in your terms infinite.
quotes.translation: 你会忽略某些信息刺激，而另一个人，例如，会立即抓住它们。即使在你的世界里，你的兴趣和欲望也充当着组织过程，筛选掉某些信息。框架二中可用的信息，用你的话来说，是无限的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "The natural person is indeed the magical person, and you have both to some extent had very recent (telepathic) examples of such activity.... Framework 2 has been a rather fascinating but mainly (underlined) hypothetical framework, in that neither of you have really been able to put it to any perceivable use in your terms. This is not to say that it has not been operating. You have not had the kind of feedback, however, that you want.
quotes.translation: "自然之人确实是魔法之人，你们两人在近期都多少有过此类活动的（心灵感应）例子……框架二一直是一个相当迷人但主要（下划线）假设性的框架，因为你们两人都未能真正将其用于你们术语中任何可感知的用途。这并不是说它没有在运作。然而，你们没有获得你们想要的那种反馈。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

source_raw.chapter_title: CHAPTER 9

quotes.text: If you believe in the laws of cause and effect, as accepted, or in the laws of polarity, as accepted (and explained in a letter we received today), then you will be bound by those laws, for they will represent your artistic technique. You will believe that you must use them in order to, say, paint the living portrait of your life. You will therefore structure your experience, drawing to yourself from Framework 2 only that which fits. You will not have the "technique" to attract other experience, and as long as you stick with one technique your life-pictures will more or less have a certain monotony.
quotes.translation: 如果你相信公认的因果律，或公认的极性法则（正如我们今天收到的一封信中所解释的），那么你将被这些法则束缚，因为它们将代表你的艺术技巧。你会相信你必须使用它们，才能，比如说，画出你生命的生动肖像。因此，你将构建你的体验，只从框架二中吸引与之相符的事物。你将没有"技巧"去吸引其他体验，而只要你坚持一种技巧，你的生命图画就会或多或少地具有某种单调性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 819, which was held last Saturday evening, had nothing to do with Mass Events. In our opinion tonight's session did, however, and for a number of reasons that will become obvious as it's read. Yet Seth didn't call it dictation.

quotes.text: Any such communications coming through the inner senses will exist in your psychological time. I have said also that this psychological time operates during sleep and during quiet times of consciousness. Now in dreams and in the dream framework you have the feeling of experiencing many hours, or even days. These days or hours that you seem to experience in dreams are not recorded by the physical body, and are outside of your physical time camouflage. If in a dream for example you experience within the framework two days, physically you do not age for these two days.
quotes.translation: 任何通过内在感官传来的此类通讯都将存在于你的心理时间中。我还说过，这种心理时间在睡眠期间和意识安静的时刻运作。现在，在梦境和梦的框架中，你有经历许多小时甚至许多天的感觉。你在梦中似乎经历的这些天或小时，并不被肉体记录，且在你的物理时间伪装之外。例如，如果你在梦中经历了框架内的两天，你的身体并不会因这两天而衰老。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Some portion of each individual is in direct contact with the very source of its own existence. Each individual is innately aware that help is available in every situation, and that information does not need to come through the physical senses alone. Many illnesses are cured, then, through quite natural methods that not only involve physical healings, but bring into play other events -- events that have great bearing on the psychological elements that may be involved behind the scenes. For those interactions we will have to look to Framework 2.
quotes.translation: 每个个体的某一部分都与其自身存在的源头直接接触。每个个体都天生知道，在任何情况下都有帮助可用，并且信息不必仅通过物理感官传来。因此，许多疾病是通过相当自然的方法治愈的，这些方法不仅涉及身体疗愈，还调动了其他事件——这些事件对可能涉及幕后心理元素有重大影响。对于这些互动，我们必须求助于框架二。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those with other intents will find excuses to leave such areas. There will be, perhaps, a chance meeting that will result in a hasty trip. On a hunch someone else might suddenly leave the area to find a new job, or decide to visit a friend in another state. Those whose experiences do not merge with nature's in that regard will not be part of that mass event. They will act on information that comes to them from Framework 2. Those who stay also act on the same information, by choosing to participate.
quotes.translation: 怀有其他意图的人会找借口离开这些区域。或许会有一场偶遇，导致一次匆忙的旅行。凭直觉，另一个人可能会突然离开该地区寻找新工作，或决定去另一个州拜访朋友。那些经历与自然在这方面不相融合的人，将不会成为那场集体事件的一部分。他们会依据从架构二收到的信息行事。而留下的人同样依据相同的信息，选择参与其中。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the same way you experience a birthday party, an automobile accident, a bridge game, or any psychological event as psychologically solid, with a smooth experienced surface that holds together in space and time. Such events, however, consist of indivisible "particles" and faster-than-light perceptions1 that never show. In other words, they contain psychic components that flow from Framework 2 into Framework 1.
quotes.translation: 同样地，你体验一场生日派对、一场车祸、一场桥牌游戏，或任何心理事件，都感觉它们像心理实体般坚实，有着在时空里连贯的平滑体验表面。然而，这类事件由不可分割的“粒子”和从未显现的超光速感知组成。换句话说，它们包含从架构二流入架构一的灵性成分。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 826, MARCH 8, 1978
9:35 P.M. WEDNESDAY

quotes.text: Some ancient religions put the existence of gods there, and saw the spirits of each living thing as existing primarily in that invisible medium of reality. Therefore, Framework 2 has always been represented in one way or another as a source of your world. Christianity saw it as heaven, inhabited by God the Father, His angels, the saints, and [the] deceased faithful.
quotes.translation: 某些古老宗教将神的存在置于那里，并将每个生物的灵魂视为主要存在于那不可见的实相媒介中。因此，架构二始终以某种方式被呈现为你世界的源头。基督教将其视为天堂，由天父、祂的天使、圣徒和已故的信徒居住。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Dictation: In the terms of our discussion, Framework 2 is the medium in which your world exists. It represents the vaster psychological reality in which your own subjective life resides.
quotes.translation: 口授：在我们讨论的术语中，架构二是你世界存在的媒介。它代表着你自身主观生活所栖居的更广大的心理实相。

quotes.text: "It is the adult's version of childhood knowledge, the human version of the animals' knowledge, the conscious version of 'unconscious' comprehension. I told you that Frameworks 1 and 2 were actually united. They seemed to be so disunited that it is almost impossible to discuss them using any other terms. To understand that much alone, to comprehend the simple idea of Framework 2's indisputable existence is strongly important, however."
quotes.translation: “它是童年知识的成人版本，动物知识的人类版本，‘无意识’理解的意识版本。我告诉过你，架构一和架构二实际上是统一的。它们显得如此不统一，以至于几乎不可能用其他术语来讨论。然而，仅理解这一点，仅领会架构二无可争议存在的简单概念，就极为重要。”
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 5. It was inevitable that Jane's images would remind me of the note I'd written well over two months ago, on re-creating the past, or updating it, through nostalgia. In Chapter 11 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 936. Her images led me to search out the collection of battered black-and-white snapshots of her that somehow, some way, she'd managed to save from her early childhood. Along with scraps of her youthful poetry, the pictures are the only physical remnants she possesses of her first years, and studying them anew I realized just how valuable they really are. I talked of having them copied and enlarged by a professional photographer; I speculated about eventually having some of them reproduced in a book. That idea may have to wait, however: For some years Jane hasn't cared to be photographed--or have pictures of herself shown, no matter when they had been taken.
source_raw.chapter_title: CHAPTER 12

quotes.text: There is, as I have told you, an inner (pause) "psychological" universe, from which your own emerges, and that inner universe is also the source of Framework 2 as well. It is responsible for all physical effects, and is behind all physical "laws."
quotes.translation: 如我告诉过你的，存在一个内在的（停顿）“心理”宇宙，你自己的世界从中浮现，而那个内在宇宙也是架构二的源头。它负责所有物理效应，并存在于所有物理“定律”背后。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Myths are natural phenomena, rising from the psyche of man as surely as giant mountain ranges emerge from the physical planet. Their deeper reality exists, however, in Framework 2 as source material for the world that you know.
quotes.translation: 现在：神话是自然现象，从人类的心灵中升起，正如巨大的山脉从物质星球上隆起一样确实。然而，它们更深层的实相存在于框架二中，作为你所知世界的原始素材。

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition
